title: centaurea kurdica subsp. ciliata
intro: Centaurea kurdica subsp. ciliata, also known as Kurdish knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Turkey. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Centaurea kurdica subsp. ciliata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antitumor activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
